title: opsago suberosa
intro: Opsago suberosa, also known as 'Encina' or 'Holm Oak', is a type of evergreen tree native to the Mediterranean region. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of a compound called oleuropein, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Opsago suberosa include its ability to help lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and protect against heart disease. Herbal preparations of Opsago suberosa include tea, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and reduce stress.
